pairs.question: What were the military ranks held by Eric Wells Morris during his career in the British Army and British Indian Army?
pairs.answer: Eric Wells Morris held the following military ranks: second lieutenant (commissioned March 1911), lieutenant (promoted during WWI), captain (promoted October 1915), major (as of June 1932), lieutenant colonel (promoted September 1935), and retired from active service in August 1939.

pairs.question: How many first-class cricket matches did Eric Wells Morris play, and what were his batting and bowling statistics?
pairs.answer: Eric Wells Morris played two first-class cricket matches. He scored a total of 46 runs with a highest score of 20 and took a single wicket.

pairs.question: What significant military honor was awarded to Eric Wells Morris, and when?
pairs.answer: Eric Wells Morris was awarded the Distinguished Service Order in the 1919 Birthday Honours.

pairs.question: What was Eric Wells Morris's educational background before joining the military?
pairs.answer: Eric Wells Morris was educated at Felsted School before proceeding to the Royal Military College, Sandhurst.

pairs.question: Which cricket teams did Eric Wells Morris represent in first-class matches?
pairs.answer: Eric Wells Morris represented the Europeans cricket team in his debut match and the Bihar team in his second match.

pairs.question: When and where did Eric Wells Morris make his first-class cricket debut?
pairs.answer: Eric Wells Morris made his first-class cricket debut in March 1929 for the Europeans cricket team against the Muslims at Lahore in the 1928–29 Lahore Tournament.

pairs.question: What was Eric Wells Morris's role at the Indian Army Service Corps (IASC) Training Establishment?
pairs.answer: Eric Wells Morris served as an instructor at the Indian Army Service Corps (IASC) Training Establishment, first in India and later at Chaklala after being reappointed in September 1933.
